title: Kung Pao Chicken

seo.title: Kung Pao Chicken Recipe: Authentic & Easy Chinese Dish
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Kung Pao Chicken with our easy recipe! Get tender chicken, crunchy peanuts, and a spicy, savory sauce. Perfect for a delicious Chinese meal at home.
longTailKeywords: how to make Kung Pao Chicken
Kung Pao Chicken step by step
best Kung Pao Chicken at home
h2Headings: Ingredients for Homemade Kung Pao Chicken
How to Make Kung Pao Chicken: Step-by-Step Instructions
Tips for the Best Kung Pao Chicken
Serving Suggestions for Kung Pao Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant plate of authentic Kung Pao Chicken with diced chicken, peanuts, chili peppers, and green onions.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this classic Chinese Kung Pao Chicken recipe, featuring tender chicken in a spicy, savory sauce.

description: Kung Pao Chicken is a vibrant and iconic Sichuan dish, celebrated for its perfect balance of spicy, sweet, sour, and savory flavors. Tender pieces of chicken are stir-fried with crunchy peanuts, fiery dried chilies, and aromatic Sichuan peppercorns, creating a truly unforgettable culinary experience. This classic Chinese lunch recipe is sure to become a favorite for its bold taste and satisfying texture.

ingredients.notes: sliced into 1-inch pieces, white and green parts separated

instructions.title: Prepare Chicken and Marinade
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the diced chicken with 2 tablespoons soy sauce, 1 tablespoon Shaoxing wine, 1 tablespoon cornstarch, and 1 teaspoon sesame oil. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly. Set aside to marinate for at least 15 minutes while you prepare other ingredients.

instructions.title: Mix the Kung Pao Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk together 2 tablespoons soy sauce, 2 tablespoons Chinkiang vinegar, 1 tablespoon granulated sugar, 1 tablespoon Shaoxing wine, 2 tablespoons chicken broth, and 1 teaspoon cornstarch until smooth. Set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare Vegetables and Aromatics
instructions.text: Dice the red and green bell peppers into 1-inch pieces. Slice the green onions into 1-inch pieces, separating the white and green parts. Mince the garlic and ginger. Cut the dried red chilies into 1-inch pieces, removing the stems.

instructions.title: Stir-fry the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat until shimmering. Add the marinated chicken in a single layer and stir-fry for 3-5 minutes, until browned and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the wok and set aside, leaving any oil behind.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Spices
instructions.text: Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the wok. Add the dried red chilies and Sichuan peppercorns. Stir-fry for about 30 seconds until fragrant and the chilies darken slightly. Be careful not to burn them. Add the minced garlic, ginger, and the white parts of the green onions. Stir-fry for another 30 seconds until fragrant.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish the Dish
instructions.text: Add the diced bell peppers to the wok and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until slightly tender-crisp. Return the cooked chicken to the wok. Give the prepared Kung Pao sauce a quick whisk, then pour it over the chicken and vegetables. Stir continuously until the sauce thickens and coats everything, about 1-2 minutes. Stir in the roasted peanuts and the green parts of the green onions. Toss to combine and serve immediately with steamed rice.

tips: For extra tender chicken, velvet the chicken by marinating it with a pinch of baking soda along with the cornstarch and soy sauce before cooking. Rinse thoroughly before stir-frying.
Adjust the spiciness to your preference by adding more or fewer dried chilies. For more heat, finely chop some of the chilies before adding them.
Always have all your ingredients prepped and ready before you start cooking. Stir-frying is a very fast process, and you won't have time to chop while cooking.
article: Kung Pao Chicken is a beloved Chinese Lunch Recipes dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
